steps.title: Identify the structure before the comma
steps.content: The blank comes after “Originally” and before “in classical Japanese,” which precedes the main clause “it has since been translated into other languages.” The opening phrase must work as a modifier of the subject “it.”

steps.title: Apply the participial phrase rule
steps.content:
An opening participial phrase uses a participle, not a finite verb.

The past participle “written” correctly creates the participial phrase “originally written in classical Japanese,” which modifies the subject “it” of the main clause.

The other three options use finite verb forms (is/was/had been), which cannot function as the head of a participial phrase.

That is the correct answer.
